commit.message:
★ UIUX 및 기능 개선, 설명 참조

## 2. 커밋 상세 내용
- **지능형 헤더 시스템 개편**
    - 페이지별(메인/통계/기록/설정) 맞춤형 타이틀 자동 전환 로직 적용
    - 미래 날짜 이동 제한(Disabled) 및 시각적 피드백(Opacity) 추가
    - 날짜 선택 모달(Picker) 호출 방식 최적화 및 안정화

- **리포트(통계) 기능 고도화**
    - 수입/지출 비중 분석을 위한 더블 도넛 차트(ApexCharts) 도입
    - 수입 vs 지출 직접 비교를 위한 바 차트(Bar Chart) 신규 배치
    - AI 금융 비서 메시지 레이아웃 및 요약 로직 개선
    - 달력 내 상세 내역 커스텀 스크롤 UI 적용

- **데이터 스토어 및 유저 설정 보강**
    - 유저 프로필 업데이트 시 비밀번호 누락 방지 로직 보완
    - 시스템 설정 및 브라우저 테마 연동(Dark/Light Mode) 기능 최적화
    - 로그아웃 시 스토어 데이터 초기화(Reset) 함수 추가

- **개발 환경 효율화 (DX 개선)**
    - `unplugin-auto-import` 도입으로 Vue/Router/Pinia 전역 오토 임포트 설정
    - 공통 버튼 클래스(`btn-primary`) 스타일 및 클릭 인터랙션 통일

* Chat - handle invalid URIs in extractCodeblockUrisFromText

When an LLM explains code that uses vscode_codeblock_uri tags, the literal
tag content may not be a valid URI, causing URI.parse to throw and crash
the chat renderer.

Add try-catch around URI.parse so invalid content gracefully returns
undefined, which callers already handle by falling back to normal code
block rendering.

commit.message:
chat: fix body tag leaking into rendered markdown when content ends with code block (#309336)

* chat: fix body tag leaking into rendered markdown when content ends with code block

When markdown content ends with a fenced code block, the closing backticks
were immediately followed by </body> on the same line, so CommonMark didn't
recognize the fence as closed. Add \n\n before </body> to ensure proper
parsing. Add regression test.

(Written by Copilot)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: delete stale snapshots for regeneration

The extra \n\n before </body> changes rendered HTML output slightly,
so snapshot baselines need to be regenerated by CI.

(Written by Copilot)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: regenerate snapshot baselines for body tag fix

Update snapshot files to reflect the extra \n\n before </body> in the
markdown template, which adds trailing whitespace before the closing
wrapper div in rendered HTML output.

(Written by Copilot)

commit.message:
fix(#216/#219): GPU alignment guard, Quick Actions tab, Resident Agent architecture (#221)

* fix(#216): GPU alignment guard, disable FunctionGemma concurrent load

- Add safeTokenCount() to avoid powers-of-2 (4096, 8192) which trigger
  LiteRT GPU reshape::Eval alignment bug on Adreno GPUs
- Disable FunctionGemma eager loading — 289MB on background thread raced
  with E4B GPU init causing OOM (lmkd kill at oom_score_adj 0)
- Add InferenceLoadingService foreground service for GPU init OOM protection
- Add GPU kernel cacheDir for serialized OpenCL compilation
- Change slider to 500-step increments (avoids power-of-2 snap points)
- Update all defaults to non-power-of-2 values (4000, 2000, 1000, 8000)
- Gate E2B/E4B model selection behind download state in settings
- Set E2B/E4B isGated=false (HuggingFace ungated since Apr 2026)
- Restore eager model loading with loading screen

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#216): request location permission at launch for weather skill

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(#216): quick actions tab with lazy FunctionGemma loading

- Add bottom navigation bar (Chats / Actions tabs)
- New ActionsScreen with FAB, bottom sheet input, action history
- New ActionsViewModel with lazy FunctionGemma init on first use
- Room: QuickActionEntity + QuickActionDao + migration v7→v8
- Bottom nav only shows on list/actions screens, not chat/settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#219): E4B-first sequential loading + Resident Agent architecture

- ChatViewModel: load E4B on GPU before FunctionGemma — full memory
  headroom during GPU kernel compilation peak (~4-5GB transient)
- ChatViewModel/initEngineWhenReady: release EmbeddingGemma only before
  E4B init; FG no longer released (E4B-first makes it safe)
- ActionsViewModel: remove unused InferenceEngine injection
- KernelNavHost: scope innerPadding to list/actions routes only — fixes
  phantom bottom padding below chat input
- specification.md: update hardware tiering table, token window note,
  skills section and roadmap to reflect Resident Agent Architecture
  (deprecate FunctionGemma, QuickIntentRouter Tier 2, E4B tool calling
  Tier 3 — see issues #218 #219 #220)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#221): address code review — mutex double-init, FG idempotency, location permission guard

- Wrap initEngineWhenReady() in gemma4InitMutex to prevent concurrent
  sendMessage() from racing during the ~20s GPU init window. Eliminates
  the double-close of EmbeddingGemma and potential 3.4GB GPU engine orphan.
- Add initMutex to FunctionGemmaRouter.initialize() so concurrent calls
  from ActionsViewModel and ChatViewModel serialize safely and are idempotent.
- Guard location permission request in MainActivity with checkSelfPermission —
  prevents the dialog firing on every onCreate() (rotation, process restart).

commit.message:
Rewrite simulator: story-driven, pipeline-integrated, all 7 scenarios

732 lines (down from bloated original). Each scenario now:
1. Tells the backstory (business requirement that led to the change)
2. Explains what will happen step-by-step
3. Triggers real ADO pipelines (az pipelines run)
4. Shows live pipeline progress (build → release)
5. Monitors health with live dashboard
6. Celebrates when SRE Agent fixes it

7 scenarios:
  1. Bad deploy crash (SCADA bug) → release trigger
  2. Bad deploy perf (checksum loop) → release trigger
  3. Bad deploy config (wrong port) → release trigger
  4. Disk pressure (VM) → Azure Monitor alert
  5. Organic load spike (no bug) → Azure Monitor alert
  6. Pipeline build failure → build failure trigger
  7. Reset all to healthy

commit.message:
Restructure: purpose-built agents with proper trigger routing

New agents:
  deployment-validator — triggered by Release pipeline (BuildSucceeded)
    Proactively checks health after every deploy, identifies which
    microservice was deployed, picks the right diagnostic skill.
  vm-ops-agent — triggered by Azure Monitor alerts (title contains 'disk')
    Handles VM infrastructure issues using disk-pressure-diagnosis skill.

Release triggers:
  Post-Deploy Validation → Release pipeline succeeds → deployment-validator
  Build Failure Investigation → Build pipeline fails → incident-handler

Incident filters:
  vm-disk-alert → title contains 'disk' → vm-ops-agent
  auto-investigate → all other Azure Monitor alerts → incident-handler

Release pipeline updated with 'services' parameter so agent can
read which microservices were deployed via GetPipelineRunVariables.

provenance verification to fail with WorkflowRefMismatch.

   distributed mesh adds 5-50ms. Full breakdown in LIMITATIONS.md.

   no mesh/crypto) vs full enterprise stack.

5. Vendor independence: Documents zero cloud dependencies in core,
   standard formats for all state, migration path.

fix: load standard MM3 parameters from .fld files

Add include_standard parameter to _mm3_import_ff, load_mm3_fld,
and ForceField.from_mm3_fld. When enabled, parses the full MM3
parameter body (rows 60-1800) in addition to the substructure
section, matching how MacroModel resolves parameters.

This fixes issue #197: the published Donoghue 2008 force field
was missing 30/38 bond and 55/78 angle parameters, causing
C-H stretches at 25,000 cm⁻¹ (should be <4,000). With standard
parameters loaded, the objective score drops from 139,911 to 9,530
(93% reduction).

The published FF still does not beat the Seminario baseline (~104)
due to MM3* functional form differences (stretch-bend coupling,
etc.) not implemented in OpenMM. Xfail gates remain in place.
